frameworks.name: anodal tDCS over left DLPFC
frameworks.one_line: noninvasive prefrontal stimulation modulating working memory performance and oscillations

frameworks.name: multichannel-optimized vs bipolar montage
frameworks.one_line: focality-optimized montage tested against classical bipolar electrode arrangement

frameworks.name: baseline-dependent response model
frameworks.one_line: stimulation effects depend on individual initial task performance

core_question: Do montage focality and individual baseline performance shape anodal tDCS effects on working memory and prefrontal oscillations?
key_assumptions: lDLPFC anodal tDCS can modulate WM and associated neural oscillations
increased focality should yield stronger behavioral effects
individual baseline performance moderates stimulation response
manipulations: stimulation type (bipolar vs multichannel vs sham) in crossover
WM load via 2-back task during stimulation
key_findings: no main effect of either montage on WM behavior or oscillations vs sham
stimulation interacted with initial task performance
low baseline performers improved and high performers worsened under multichannel stimulation
induced task-related theta power scaled with baseline WM ability
interpretations: tDCS effects on WM are baseline-dependent rather than uniformly facilitatory
focality alone does not guarantee stronger behavioral or oscillatory effects
limitations_noted: no group-level main effect detected with classical comparison
results inconsistent with several prior lDLPFC tDCS studies
